item.title: Schecter dUg Pinnick DP-12 LH bass in Satin Black — $1,549
item.description:
$1,549 · Brand New · Studio Gears

Schecter dUg Pinnick DP-12 String Lefty Bass Satin Black

The dUg Pinnick DP-12 delivers a fast, comfortable playing experience through its thin 'C' neck profile and ebony fretboard, engineered specifically for left-handed players seeking extended range and precision. This 12-string bass features Seymour Duncan active electronics and a mahogany body with chrome hardware, combining playability with articulate tone shaping. Built in South Korea to Schecter's specifications, it ships fully factory setup and ready to perform.

Playability & Construction

The three-piece maple neck with carbon fiber reinforcement rods maintains structural integrity while the thin 'C' shape and 16" fretboard radius facilitate smooth position shifts across 22 X-Jumbo frets. A 34" scale with graphite nut (52mm width) and Grover tuners ensure stable intonation, while Luminlay glow-in-the-dark side dot markers provide visibility during low-light playing—complemented by pearloid dot inlays with a DUg logo at the 12th fret.

Tone & Electronics

The Seymour Duncan Pro Active APB-1 pickup paired with a 2-band active EQ (STC-2S-B0) offers responsive midrange and treble control, powered by a 9-volt clip-in battery compartment for consistent output. The Schecter Custom Bass bridge with bolt-on mahogany body construction delivers focused low-end punch suitable for both fingerstyle and pick articulation across 12 strings.

Key Features
  • Left-handed configuration with 12-string setup
  • Mahogany body with bolt-on maple 3-piece neck reinforced with carbon fiber rods
  • Ebony fretboard with 22 X-Jumbo frets and 16" radius
  • 34" scale length (864mm) with thin 'C' neck profile
  • Graphite nut, 2.047" width
  • Seymour Duncan Pro Active APB-1 bridge pickup
  • Active 2-band Seymour Duncan STC-2S-B0 EQ with volume control
  • Grover tuners with 2-way adjustable truss rod
  • Luminlay glow-in-the-dark side dot markers
  • Chrome hardware and Schecter Custom Bass bridge
  • Pearloid dot inlays with DUg logo at 12th fret
  • Satin Black finish, made in South Korea

This 12-string bass combines extended range with left-handed accessibility and professional-grade electronics, making it a capable instrument for genres demanding harmonic depth and dynamic control.

Great condition. Two small scratches on the back of the body (see last two photos).

item.title: Overwater Scott Devine Signature Custom 5 - Spalted Beech Natural Satin — $6,414.31
item.description:
$6,414.31 · Brand New · Bass Freaks

In stock now: Overwater Scott Devine Signature Custom 5 - Spalted Beech Natural Satin. Buyer out of EU responsible for all import cost.

Specs

  • Weight: 4.50 kg / 9.9 lbs
  • Model: Scott Devine Signature Custom 5, Signature Series
  • Serial number: 26-4506
  • Strings: 5
  • Scale: 33" medium scale
  • Frets: 24 medium
  • Neck: 3-piece wenge
  • Neck joint: extended bolt-on
  • Fingerboard: rosewood
  • Body: white ash
  • Top: spalted beech
  • Finish: Natural Satin
  • Pickups: Overwater soapbar
  • Electronics: active/passive Overwater/East 3-band EQ with variable mid and passive tone
  • Bridge: Overwater mono-rail with through-body stringing with 19 mm spacing
  • Tuners: Overwater enclosed machines
  • Knobs: Overwater turned black

The Scott Devine Signature Custom is the bass Scott Devine designed together with Overwater to match his own playing. Scott is the founder of Scott's Bass Lessons (ScottsBassLessons.com / SBL) and the SBL Academy, the largest online educational community for bass players in the world. Hand-built in the Overwater workshops in Cumbria, this 5-string pairs a three-piece wenge neck and white ash body under a spalted beech top, with a pair of Overwater soapbar pickups running into the active/passive Overwater/East three-band preamp with variable mid control and passive tone. In stock at Bass Freaks, boutique Overwater dealer in Europe.

item.title: Jackson X Series Concert Bass CBXNT DX IV 2022 - Absynthe Frost — $511.71
item.description:
$511.71 · Excellent · Centre Musical EMF

The Jackson X Series Concert Bass CBXNT DX IV is a modern electric bass designed for players seeking power, precision, and playing comfort. Its neck-through construction provides excellent sustain and stability, while the PJ pickup configuration combined with an active 3-band EQ delivers a wide tonal range, from deep low end to punchy attack. Made in Indonesia as part of Jackson’s X Series lineup, it features an aggressive look and fast playability, making it ideal for rock, metal, and modern styles.


Features

• Poplar body

• Neck-through maple neck with graphite reinforcement rods

• Laurel fingerboard with compound 12”–16” radius

• 24 jumbo frets with sharkfin inlays

• 34” scale length

• PJ pickup configuration (Precision + Jazz)

• Active preamp with 3-band EQ (bass, mid, treble)

• Controls: volume, pickup blend, and 3-band EQ

• Jackson Bass Bacher IV string-through-body bridge

• Jackson sealed die-cast tuners

• Chrome hardware

Condition

• Excellent condition

• Year: 2022

• Made in Indonesia

• Serial number: 2916654618

item.title: Gretsch Electromatic CVT Bass 2025 - Present - Bristol Fog — $280
item.description:
$280 · Excellent · Del's Gear Outlet

Gretsch Electromatic CVT / Bristow Fog Finish / Mint / All Original With High Mass Bridge and D'addario Flatwounds

A STATEMENT-MAKING MEDIUM SCALE BASS

Experience the playability of a short-scale bass with the tonal range of a full-scale model in the Gretsch Electromatic CVT medium-scale electric bass. With its 32-inch scale length, this versatile instrument offers a comfortable playing experience without sacrificing the warm, resonant tone delivered by its solid mahogany body and bolt-on mahogany neck. A laurel fingerboard with a 12-inch radius and 20 frets adds character and smooth playability, while a true bone nut, 4-saddle tailpiece, and die-cast sealed tuners provide excellent tuning stability and sustain. Equipped with two Low Down alnico pickups — a single-coil at the bridge and a humbucker at the neck — the Electromatic CVT delivers a wide tonal palette. Independent volume controls and a master tone knob allow for precise shaping of its signature Gretsch sound. Long favored by discerning players, medium-scale basses have amassed quite a loyal following. With the Gretsch Electromatic CVT, you too can experience the perfect blend of comfort, tone, and classic Gretsch character.


Classic style meets modern substance

For decades, stages the world over have been graced with iconic Gretsch style. The Electromatic CVT certainly has that moxie in spades. Substantially crafted with a mahogany body and bolt-on Performance C mahogany neck, the brunt of the bass’s strong, resonant tone is further complemented by the laurel fingerboard that delivers a classically smooth playing surface and caps off the instrument's all-around classic look. Further enhanced with a 4-saddle tailpiece, authentic bone nut, die-cast sealed tuners, and Neo-classical thumbnail inlays, the Electromatic CVT is equal parts elegance and efficiency.


A Prudent Pairing

Low Down alnico bass pickups are known for their gloriously punchy sound and ability to play right in the pocket across a variety of styles and genres. But pair a Low Down single-coil with a humbucker and you receive a vast palette of low-frequency coloration. From deep, thunderous plucks to silky smooth grooves, the individual volume controls let you shape each pickup’s output, while the master tone knob adds the finishing touch, giving you full command over a sound that’s distinctly your own.


Gretsch Electromatic CVT Features:

  • 32-in. medium-scale length delivers the playability of a short-scale model and the full tone of a standard-scale bass
  • Mahogany body and bolt-on Performance C mahogany neck provide a solid feel, ample resonance, and a balanced tone
  • Laurel fingerboard with a 12-in. radius and 20 frets offers excellent playability
  • Low Down alnico single-coil bridge pickup and Low Down alnico humbucking neck pickup yield a spectrum of bass tones
  • Bone nut and die-cast sealed tuners provide substantial tuning stability
  • 2 volume knobs grant control over each pickup while a master tone knob allows you to further sculpt your tone

Includes the Guitar only with no case or gig bag. Shipped with the original Factory carton.

item.title: 【new】Sire / F10dc 6 NT 4.814kg #2N26A90027【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $3,235
item.description:
$3,235 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Sire became famous for releasing basses supervised by Marcus Miller. (Titles omitted)


The quality of the guitars and basses manufactured by the company is extremely high, and they are widely supported not only by Miller's fans but also by many others.


This is the F10dc 6-string model, which pursues the highest level of modern style within the brand, which is strongly associated with the JB style.


The body top is made of rosewood, and the back is made of alder.


The double-cut body shape not only has a modern and flowing look, but also allows for smooth access to the higher positions.


The neck is a 5-piece through-neck construction combining hard maple and mahogany.


This method of laminating multiple pieces of wood to create the neck reduces left-right and individual differences, resulting in higher rigidity and stability.


It uses a 35-inch scale, which enhances the tension in the low-end and improves pitch clarity.


The fingerboard is made of rosewood.


The relatively flat 12-inch radius fingerboard and fast-attacking stainless steel frets maintain a consistent playability even in the higher positions.


It responds with clear articulation even in modern playing techniques such as slap bass and fast phrases.


The fingerboard features an edgeless design with smoothly rounded edges, fitting the style of players who prioritize smooth fingering.


Most players will likely find this feature appealing.


The pickups are the original Marcus Custom-H Revolution Set humbucking pickups with wooden covers.


A variation switch allows you to switch the pickup wiring, enabling a wide range of tones, from thick and powerful humbucker tones to clear and articulate single-coil-like sounds.


The preamp is the Marcus Heritage-3 HD, boasting a wide range and clear output.


Combined with the pickup variation switch, it allows for a wide range of sound shaping.


The sound is characterized by a deep, tight low end and rich sustain.


While possessing the powerful push characteristic of humbuckers, the treble is moderately mellow and not harsh on the ears.


The separation of each frequency range is excellent, maintaining a consistent tension and resolution from Low-B to High-C, so chords never sound muddy.


The rich harmonics and stable pitch make it highly compatible with technical soloing and modern ensembles that prioritize clarity.


This is an outstanding instrument that matches styles that want to fully utilize the rich sound characteristic of high-end basses and the wide tonal range of multi-string basses.


SPECIFICATION


-BODY-

Body Material: Alder with Rosewood Top

Body Shape: Modern Double Cutaway Bass Shape

Body Color: NT (Natural)


-NECK-

Neck Material: Hard Maple / Mahogany 5-pc

Neck Shape: C-Shape

Scale: 35""

Fingerboard Material: Rosewood with Edgeless™ (Rolled Fretboard Edges)

Fingerboard Radius: 12""

Frets: 2.4mm Medium, 24 Stainless Frets

String Nut: Bone Nut, 54mm Width

Inlay: WH Pearloid / Abalone Rounded-Rectangular Block

Neck Joint: Neck Thru


-ELECTRONICS-

Pickup: Marcus Custom-H Revolution Set (Wooden Cover)

Preamp: Marcus Heritage-3 HD

Controls: Volume / Tone, Blender, Treble, Middle / Freq, Bass, Pickup Variation SW

Knobs: Aluminum Knob Black

-HARDWARE-

Bridge: New Marcus Heavymass Custom

Tuning Gear: Premium Diecasting Gear

Hardware Finish: Black Satin

Pickguard: None

-CASE-

HARDCASE


Delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.


Accessories: Hard case, warranty card, wrench


Text: Kaigo Kawami


Product Photos: Muragishi


Manufacturer's warranty included


◯GIB, TONIQ Warranty Information


●Lifetime Free Maintenance


Instruments purchased from our store are eligible for lifetime free ""String Vibration Setup"" (normally ¥4,400). (Reservation required)


・Please present your warranty card with your name filled in.


・Only applicable to the person who purchased the instrument at a GIB or TONIQ physical store or online shop.


・Please bear the cost of shipping and other miscellaneous expenses.



● Guaranteed Buyback Price


If you sell your purchased item within 6 months of purchase, we guarantee a buyback price. The price is 50% for new items and 70% for used items.


● Warranty Against Defects


Basic Co., Ltd. provides a warranty against defects for products sold by the company. Excluding some items such as consigned goods, we will repair (or refund if repair is not possible) any malfunctions that occur during normal use within a certain warranty period from the date of purchase free of charge.


The warranty period is 3 months for used items and 1 year for new items.


Please note that the following cases will incur a charge even within the warranty period:


* Malfunctions and damage caused by transportation, movement, dropping, etc.


* Replacement of consumables.


* Malfunctions and damage caused by fire, earthquake, salt damage, gas damage, or abnormal voltage.


* Malfunctions and damage caused by handling contrary to the precautions described in the instruction manual.


* Malfunctions or damage to parts repaired or modified by parties other than our company.


* If the warranty card does not have your name, model name, serial number, etc. filled in. Or, if the wording has been altered.


* If the warranty certificate is not presented or is lost.


(Warranty is only valid for purchases made within Japan.)


● Up to 15% OFF with Trade-in


When you purchase an item, you will receive a 10% discount on the sales price when you use our trade-in service. (Consignment items are excluded. This discount applies only if the trade-in value is 15% or more of the sales price.)


Furthermore, if you trade in an instrument eligible for our monthly buyback campaign, the discount rate will be 15%.


We ship with sufficient packaging.


We also ship internationally.



●Price includes VAT & Duties & Shipping Fee

Free Shipping Worldwide. All import duties and taxes are included in the price. There are no hidden fees upon delivery.


If you return a product, you will be responsible for all shipping and customs fees.


Note: All electronic devices, such as effect pedals and amplifiers, are set for use in Japan.

Please be sure to check the voltage and power specifications of your country before purchasing and using.

item.title: YAMAHA USED BB735A (Indigo Blue) — $687.87
item.description:
$687.87 · Good · Ikebe Music

Our Business Hours is Mon to Fri / 11:00 to 18:00 JPN time
We will close Sat and Sun, so we will reply to inquiry that received weekend on next week.
Your understanding will be appreciated.

We are receiving the customer order with first comes first serves basis both on website and at real shop.
So occasionally it will be sold out while having email communication.
Please understand in advance.

Japanese consumption tax is not required for international shopping. However you may be required to pay duty, tax and import fee when receiving your items in your country. You must pay all these duty, tax and fee as receiver/importer and we do not cover any of them.

■Please note
The product description is an automatic translation of the Japanese description. Please contact us if you have any questions.

-----------------------------------

Please be noted this one is USED not new.
There is not any issue on the function and the playing condition but blemishes on the cosmetic.
Please send message for more detail information of the condition.

-----------------------------------

Used active bottom brackets have arrived!

The BB735A is a renewed active 5-string model that inherits the identity of the BB series!

With the modern music scene in mind, the Yamaha BB bass has been boldly revamped under the concept of "the ultimate bolt-on passive." This is the BB735, a revamped variation model that faithfully inherits the basic specifications of the top-of-the-line BBP model, and furthermore, adopts an active specification for the first time in a long time for the BB model, which is predominantly passive. It achieves high cost performance through overseas manufacturing!

This guitar inherits the wood materials and laminate construction of the higher-end BB models, featuring a three-layer laminate body of alder/maple/alder, a five-piece laminate neck combining maple and mahogany for a slim grip shape, and a rosewood fingerboard. The body, neck, and headstock are finished in matte black, and the various parts are finished in black nickel. It features high-grade Alnico 5 magnet PJ pickups and a black 3-ply pickguard. The pickup covers use a general-purpose design and can be customized to your liking.
This active bass features a control section with volume, pickup balancer, and 3-band EQ, and a switch allows you to switch between active and passive modes, enabling you to choose the sound that suits the situation. Additionally, a battery alert LED is located on the back, allowing you to easily see when it's time to change the battery.

There are some minor scratches, but no large, noticeable dents, and it is in relatively good condition. There is what appears to be a dent on the elbow area, but it is probably just part of the wood grain.
The truss rod has plenty of adjustment range, and the neck is in good condition.

Weight ≈ 4.48 kg
Accessories: Original gig bag

The American Vintage Series Jazz Bass remains highly popular to this day.

With its resonant alder tone, warm and clear sound from the two single-coil pickups, and versatile expression thanks to the front and rear volume controls, this bass delivers a sound worthy of its timeless classic status. This particular instrument appears to be a 1996 model, commemorating Fender's 50th anniversary. While the neck and pickup pocket have date markings, the potentiometer date is 1996. The 50th anniversary mark on the back of the headstock confirms its 1996 manufacture date.

There are paint chips, scratches, and tarnishing on the metal parts. There is a large dent on the fretboard near the 18th fret of the 1st string. The strap pin on the back of the headstock and one pickguard screw are missing, and the bridge has been replaced from the original to a similar type. The electronics appear to be original.
The truss rod is stiff in the tightening direction, but we have confirmed that there is still room for adjustment within the normal range. The neck condition is slightly upward-rising, but we have leveled it in our shop so that it can be used without any buzzing.

Weight ≈ 4.28 kg
Accessories: Original hard case

A used "TRBX605FM" with a flame maple top has arrived!

This guitar features a flame maple top and an alder body, resulting in a luxurious look and a more focused mid-low range. The three-dimensional cut of the body ensures sufficient volume while maintaining a lightweight and comfortable fit, producing powerful bass tones. The neck employs a five-piece maple/mahogany construction that is highly resistant to warping and twisting, ensuring high strength. The joint shape, designed after extensive testing, does not compromise playability in the higher positions and efficiently transmits string vibrations.
Equipped with a 3-band EQ preamp and an active/passive switch, it allows you to switch between a delicate and warm passive sound and a clear, versatile active sound. It also features a battery alert on the back of the body that lights up when the battery is low to indicate it's time to replace it.

There are some minor scratches, but no large, noticeable dents, and it is in relatively good condition.
The truss rod has plenty of adjustment range, and the neck condition is good with no major issues.

■Slim shape delivers surprisingly powerful bass. The body, with its three-dimensional cuts, achieves lightweight construction and a comfortable fit while maintaining sufficient volume to produce powerful bass. The body is made of mahogany, resulting in a warm and resonant bass sound.

■The slimmest neck among Yamaha basses. The easy-to-grip neck allows for smooth fingering and supports even the most difficult playing styles. It features a 5-piece maple/mahogany construction that is resistant to warping and twisting. The joint shape, designed after repeated long-term testing, ensures high strength and transmits string vibrations efficiently without compromising playability in the higher positions.

■The newly developed TRBX300 series active circuit, designed to stimulate bassists' motivation, features a new "Performance EQ" function that allows for instant switching between five EQ settings, making it easy for beginners to use. Professional musicians have repeatedly tested it with various playing techniques, and presets for slap/pick/flat/finger/solo are available that can be used directly in live performances and recordings. You can get sounds that suit various playing techniques with a single touch, and by using the 2-band EQ in conjunction, you can create even more precise sounds.

Spirit is a direct descendant brand of Steinberger.

This model is the XT-25, a headless bass that symbolizes Steinberger.

The body features a quilted maple top and a basswood back.

The neck is made of 3-piece hard maple, and the fingerboard is engineered hardwood.

The vibrant red color and the three-dimensional quilted maple top create a striking stage-ready look.

It is equipped with two original humbucker-type pickups.

Based on the warm and full sound characteristic of passive pickups, it outputs a straightforward and well-balanced tone.

The excellent weight balance, thanks to the headless construction, is also a major attraction.

It achieves high playability and excellent handling.

This is a classic model that has been loved for a long time, possessing the practicality that is characteristic of Steinberger.

The frets have approximately 75% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork have some minor scratches, but overall it is in good condition.

item.title: Fender Jazz Bass 1965 - Dakota Red — $8,597.15
item.description:
$8,597.15 · Good · ROCKBOX

Fender Jazz Bass from '65, except for the body, which is from 1963. One of the tuning machines has been replaced with a Fender tuner, maintaining its original aesthetic. The four-screw neck plate has also been replaced with one from the '70s and the pickguard is not vintage. This incredible instrument was refinished in Dakota Red nitro laquer many years ago, giving it a beautiful natural relic that makes it even more attractive. An authentic vintage bass that sounds and tunes amazing, has a smooth feel, and is in very, very good condition to play.


This unit doesn't have a case, but if you purchase online, we'll ship it in an old Fender case with some broken latches, but functional enough for its safe journey to its new home.

item.title: FUJIGEN(FGN) NJB100MBAH -White Blonde- #J250024[4.29kg][Made In Japan] — $1,023.29
item.description:
$1,023.29 · Brand New · Kurosawa Music Ikebukuro

----- *Important Notice!!


To prevent fraudulent purchases, PayPal guest checkout is not available.


Please create a PayPal account before payment.

*It appears possible to pay by credit card on the Reverb marketplace without using PayPal; however, this actually processes the transaction as a PayPal guest checkout, and the order is not valid.

-----

Welcome to Kurosawa Ikebukuro! We ship worldwide!!


Thank you for viewing!


Please feel free to contact us with any questions.


Also, please carefully read the shop policy located below the guitar description.


-----------------------------------------

We’ve just received a classic-style bass featuring an ash body and maple fretboard!



【Sound】

While the ash and maple combination produces a distinct, clear tone, the overall sound is pleasing to the ear.

It’s perfect for enjoying 70s-style funk and slap bass lines.

It delivers that quintessential "golden era" ash-and-maple sound—earthy, raw, and full of character.

Thanks to the passive electronics, playing softly yields a soft, upright-bass-like nuance, while playing hard unleashes the muscular, punchy "snap" and growl characteristic of ash.

Roll off the tone control, and you can achieve a thick, old-school Motown sound; that kind of versatility is a true hallmark of passive electronics.



・Bridge Pickup (Rear)

The sound is incredibly tight, making you want to dive straight into a funk groove.

If you’re laying down 16th-note patterns, you’ll instantly feel like a funk machine.


・Neck Pickup (Front)

It produces a bold, thick tone with a wild, aggressive edge—great for digging in with a pick.

It’s also highly recommended for strumming to get a bouncy, dynamic feel.


A standout feature is the "punch" you get from the "Turbo Switch" integrated into the tone knob.

The moment you pull the knob, the low-mids surge forward, delivering a powerful sound reminiscent of a Precision Bass, but with a more modern edge.

Despite being passive, it never feels like it lacks power.





【Playability】

The neck features a rounded "U-shape" profile—neither too thin nor too thick.

Because it isn't excessively thin, it offers great stability and is designed to minimize fatigue during long playing sessions.

It remains stable without requiring excessive thumb pressure, helping to prevent unnecessary strain.


The finish is a high-gloss coat.

It offers a grip that feels natural in the hand; even during energetic stage performances, your left hand won't slip too much, allowing you to hold your position firmly.

It’s also resistant to grime; a quick wipe with a cloth after playing is all it takes to keep it looking brand new. The fretboard features side binding, which helps prevent fret sprout and ensures a smooth feel against your fingers—a welcome detail.


The instrument is finished with a durable urethane coating that stands up well to environmental changes, allowing you to focus on your playing without worrying about the guitar's condition, no matter where you take it.

◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿ ◆



【Specifications】

1. Wood Configuration

Ash body, maple fretboard, maple neck.


2. Electronics

Equipped with Fujigen original pickups.

Delivers a versatile sound suitable for any genre.

The control layout is a standard configuration: 2 Volume and 1 Tone.

Pulling up the Tone knob switches the wiring, allowing you to choose between series and parallel connections.

This single instrument offers the tonal range of two.


3. Patented Technology: "C.F.S. (Circle Fretting System)"

Once you experience the clear pitch and the excellent note separation when playing chords, you’ll be hooked.

It is highly acclaimed for its clear resonance and extended sustain. ◆＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿＿ ◆

Scale: 34" (864mm)

Tuners: GOTOH GB2

Bridge: GOTOH 203B-4 (String Pitch = 19mm)

Controls: 2 Volume, 1 Tone with Push-Pull Switch (Series/Parallel Selector)

item.title: 【used】Fender / American Ultra Jazz Bass® V Mocha Burst 2022 4.50kg #US22033700【GIB Hyogo】【Plek-adjusted】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $3,140
item.description:
$3,140 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

This item has been leveled using PLEK.

The string height at the 12th fret will be 1.5mm for the G string and 2.0mm for the E string (2.3mm for the Low-B string).

Fender is, of course, the number one brand of electric guitars and basses.

It is loved by countless top artists such as Jaco Pastorious, Marcus Miller, and Geddy Lee. (Titles omitted)

The Jazz Bass was released as a higher-end model than the Precision Bass.

Its sharp and clear sound is loved by many bassists, and it continues to receive overwhelming support and shines brightly even today.

This instrument is a 5-string model from Fender's advanced modern line, the American Ultra series, which inherits the lineage of the American Deluxe series that once took the world by storm.

It features a more refined and compact body design, and is equipped with a noiseless and powerful active circuit, among other updates for modern players.

This bass features a classic combination of an alder body and a rosewood fingerboard.

The neck area incorporates technology designed to maximize playability.

The neck shape is a Modern D shape, which gradually changes shape from the lower to the higher positions.

Furthermore, the fingerboard combines a 10" to 14" Compound Radius, which flattens towards the higher positions, allowing for extremely smooth fingering that belies the thickness of a 5-string bass neck.

The neck heel features a bold taper, providing stress-free access to the 21st fret, a significant advantage.

The pickups are Ultra Noiseless Vintage Jazz Bass pickups, which extract the best of Fender's tone while minimizing noise.

The preamp uses an original 18V powered circuit, achieving overwhelming clarity with wide headroom and versatile sound shaping with a powerful and effective EQ.

A mini-switch allows for instant switching to passive mode. The sound differs from the traditional vintage Fender feel; it features a very cohesive and well-balanced tone, almost as if it were mastered from the start.
You can directly enjoy the noiseless, clear resonance of the 5-string, making it an immediate asset in an ensemble.

For those who prefer the modern Fender sound of American Standard or American Professional, this is a sophisticated instrument that will undoubtedly be a good match.

item.title: No Name Music Man Stingray style bass guitar 2010's - Tobacco Sunburst — $352.24
item.description:
$352.24 · Excellent · Lockers Gear Locker

This was made in China for a middle east music store and used to have a 'TMC' logo on the headstock.

Despite that, it plays and sounds really good and I had a couple of pro players ask me where it came from as they were really surprised at the build quality and the sound.

Its a perfectly good instrument and would suit someone looking to start playing bass or working within a budget.

Comes with a pro grade hard case and a wide leather strap.

item.title: Wolfe Fretless 4 string Bass Guitar Unknown - Varnished — $835.83
item.description:
$835.83 · Fair · Lockers Gear Locker

This guitar is a real mystery!

I found it covered in dust and cobwebs at the back of a London music store a few years ago and have tried to track down any history about it to no avail.

It is obviously a well used professional instrument and, judging by the war wounds, buckle rash and state of the electrics, it has been adapted, modified and customised to someone's particular playing style and played long and hard on stage but well loved.

The body and neck are maple with a mahogany veneer front and an ebony fingerboard.

All gold plated metal work with machine heads saying 'Made in Germany'.

Twin pick ups that appear to be hand wound and it features a built in pre-amp.

The most unexpected thing about it is the sound. Its phenomenal!

There are positional fret markers on the side of the neck but it is entirely fretless.

If anyone can shed any light on it, I would be delighted to know.

Comes with a generic pro quality hard case and wide cloth strap and will be appreciated by any bass player looking to move onto a fretless.

item.title: 【used】Ibanez / MDM1606 2026 4.19kg #I260200906【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,734
item.description:
$1,734 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Ibanez is a leading Japanese guitar and bass brand, globally renowned alongside Fender and Gibson.

This is the MDM1606, a 6-string model that incorporates the brand's accumulated expertise in multi-string basses and practical specifications.

The body top features eye-catching Poplar Burl with an exotic grain, while the back is made of Alder.

The neck is made of Roasted Maple, which has been heat-treated to enhance the wood's stability.

Furthermore, a graphite reinforcement rod is built into the neck, providing extremely high rigidity against strong tension and environmental changes.

It features a C-shaped neck profile exclusive to the MDM series, offering exceptional comfort.

The neck heel is recessed and rounded for easy access to the higher positions, and fluorescent dots are embedded in the side of the fretboard.

The fretboard is made of Rosewood and features highly visible white dot inlays.

The frets are made of medium-sized stainless steel for superior wear resistance. It maintains a fast, clear sound response and smooth playing feel for slides and vibrato over a long period of time.

The bridge features an MR5S bridge with an independent structure for each string.
The saddles can be moved ±1.5mm left and right, allowing for arbitrary adjustment of the string spacing.
This player-oriented design comfortably accommodates a variety of playing techniques such as slap and fingerpicking.

The pickups are passive Delta S6J type, mounted in the front and rear positions.
They employ neodymium magnets, stainless steel pole pieces, and a hum-canceling structure, producing a punchy sound that firmly supports the foundation of the ensemble.

The preamp is an Ibanez Sonitech S Preamp.

It delivers a natural sound without excessive coloration of the original tone, allowing for a wide range of sound shaping in combination with the master tone.

It also features an EQ bypass function via push/pull operation of the volume knob.

Furthermore, the combination of the 3-way Mid frequency switch and the unique Hexa Mid system is particularly noteworthy. The switch on the back panel allows you to switch between two modes as follows:

* H (High) mode: 350Hz, 600Hz, 1kHz

* L (Low) mode: 250Hz, 450Hz, 850Hz

This allows you to access a total of six different mid-range frequencies using the 3-way switch, and precisely control them with the mid-range knob.

Because you can pinpoint and adjust the mid-range frequencies that affect the clarity of the bass, you can create a tone that fits perfectly into any genre or ensemble.

The sound is characterized by clear pitch from the Low-B on the 6th string to the High-C on the 1st string, combining a wide range with a tight, fast attack.

No matter where you play across the wide range of the multi-string bass, the separation of notes is excellent, making it highly compatible with everything from technical solos with many notes to heavy, modern ensembles.

This sophisticated 6-string bass boasts high playability to meet the demanding requirements of players and features comprehensive hardware for a wide range of sound shaping.

Fret life is approximately 90%.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork are quite clean overall, although there are some small dents on the back of the body.

The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were observed.

Controls: Master Volume, Balancer, Master Tone (Pull-Preamp Bypass), 3-way Mid Frequency Switch, Bass, Middle, Treble, Hexa Mid System (Back Panel)
Nut Width: 51.7mm
Bridge String Spacing: 18mm (Variable)
Scale Length: 34 Inch
Year of Manufacture: 2026
Country of Manufacture: INDONESIA

Squier, a brand under the Fender umbrella, offers a wide range of models with excellent cost performance.

With its 1970s style featuring block inlays and binding, and a special color exclusive to the FSR, this is a truly unique instrument.

It is equipped with two Duncan Designed JB101 Single Coil pickups.

Based on the clear and articulate sound characteristic of a Jazz Bass, it combines power and ease of playability.

The Vintage Modified series is a particularly highly regarded and popular series within Squier.

It boasts a good balance of vintage-like looks and sound, and excellent playability, offering a level of quality that exceeds its price.

◯GIB and TONIQ Warranty Information

●Permanent Free Maintenance

Instruments purchased from our store are eligible for permanent free "String Vibration Setup" (normally ¥4,400). (Reservation required)

・Please present your warranty card with your name filled in.

・Only applicable to the person who purchased the instrument at a GIB or TONIQ physical store or online shop.

・Please bear the cost of shipping and other miscellaneous expenses.

●Re-purchase Price Guarantee

If you sell your purchased item within 6 months of purchase, we guarantee the buyback price. The price is 50% for new items and 70% for used items.

●Defect Warranty

Basic Co., Ltd. provides a warranty against defects for products sold by the company. With the exception of some items such as consigned goods, we will repair (or refund if repair is not possible) any malfunctions that occur during normal use within a certain warranty period from the date of purchase free of charge.

The warranty period is 3 months for used products and 1 year for new products.

Please note that the following cases will incur a charge even within the warranty period:

* Malfunctions and damage caused by transportation, movement, dropping, etc.

* Replacement of consumables.

* Malfunctions and damage caused by fire, earthquake, salt damage, gas damage, or abnormal voltage.

* Malfunctions and damage caused by handling contrary to the precautions described in the instruction manual.

* Malfunctions or damage to parts repaired or modified by a party other than our company.

* If the warranty certificate does not have your name, model name, serial number, etc. filled in, or if the wording has been altered.

* If the warranty certificate is not presented or is lost.

(Warranty is only provided for purchases made in Japan.)

● Up to 15% OFF with Trade-in

When you use our trade-in service at the time of purchase, the sales price will be 10% off. (Consignment items are excluded. This offer is only applicable if the trade-in value is 15% or more of the selling price.)

Furthermore, if you trade in an instrument eligible for our monthly buyback campaign, the discount rate will be 15%.

We will ship with sufficient packaging.

We also ship internationally.

item.title: Fender PB62-80 Precision Bass - JV Series — $1,967.33
item.description:
$1,967.33 · Very Good · Found Sound

Fender PB62-80 Precision Bass with Olympic white finish, Made in Japan, with a JV serial number. The JV era of Fender marks the period where USA production standards were declining, so they singed a deal to produce Fender branded instruments made by luthiers in Japan whose production standards were nothing but excellence.

Alder body and maple neck with a rosewood fretboard, 34" scale, 7.25" radius, vintage C shape. Original Fender USA PB-Vintage split single coil pickups. Pots have been replaced (original included), ashtray set and thumb rest installed by previous owner.

This bass reflects the care and consideration that was put into it, with decently low action and flat wound strings, it plays remarkably well. Neck date 9 Oct 1984, Fujigen Gakki factory.

Includes a Fender hardshell case. Serviced before sale, setup with flatwound strings and ashray covers, thumb rest added. Original pots included but upgraded pots installed. Original pickups.

Found Sound Australia Pty Ltd.
110 Albert St
Brunswick East, VIC 3057
Australia

Its reputation for powerful sound and reliable construction makes it a sought-after choice for both aspiring and professional musicians.

item.title: 【used】YAMAHA / TRBX605FM 2025 4.03kg #ILN123090【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $919
item.description:
$919 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Yamaha is a leading Japanese brand that has supported the music scene both domestically and internationally since the early days of guitar and bass manufacturing in Japan. It is truly a brand that Japan can be proud of.

Top musicians who transcend time, borders, and genres, including Nathan East, the next-generation technician Vincen García (who has recently gained explosive popularity worldwide), and Seiji Kameda in Japan, all use Yamaha basses (titles omitted).

The reason why so many renowned artists choose Yamaha instruments is undoubtedly due to the overwhelming quality of Japanese craftsmanship that the Yamaha brand has built up over many years, and the unwavering reliability it provides in the field.

While Yamaha boasts many famous models such as the SBV, SG, and BB, the TRB series is equally beloved.

The body top features beautifully grained Flame Maple, and the back is made of well-balanced Alder.

It boasts an elegant look.

The neck features a robust 5-piece construction combining maple and mahogany.

This technique of laminating multiple pieces of wood together minimizes left-right and individual variations, resulting in greater stability.

The fingerboard is rosewood.

The pickups are original dual-coil pickups using Alnico V magnets.

They deliver a low-noise, clear tone, from deep bass to high frequencies in the upper register, ensuring a clear sound image even when playing chords.

The original 3-band EQ is highly effective, allowing for significant tone control with intuitive operation.

From punchy slap sounds to singing solo tones with emphasized mids, it instantly enables versatile sound shaping to suit various ensembles.

The modern and refined body design combines smooth playability with excellent instrument balance.

Furthermore, this instrument features a white pearl pickguard fitted as a slap step.

By filling the gap between the strings and the body, it prevents fingers from getting too far under the strings, improving playability. While being low-noise, it possesses an overwhelming power that firmly supports the foundation of the ensemble.

With minimal muddiness in the bass, the clear, well-defined, and fast-paced Hi-Fi tone faithfully follows everything from beautiful piano-like chord playing to high-speed slap bass.

This guitar has the potential to excel in any genre, from pop accompaniment to intense rock and technically demanding fusion.

The frets have approximately 90% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork have a pickguard attached. There are no noticeable scratches.

The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were observed.

Seymour Duncan is extremely famous as a pickup brand, but their bass guitars are also quite popular.

This is the DJ-110M '75.

This is a Jazz Bass from the Traditional Series, meticulously assembled in a domestic workshop (ESP), bearing the name of Seymour Duncan, a world-renowned pickup manufacturer.

This instrument faithfully respects the specifications of a 1975 Jazz Bass.

The body is made of ash, and the neck and fingerboard are made of maple. The neck binding and block inlays give it a cool, refined look characteristic of the 70s.

Naturally, it comes standard with genuine Seymour Duncan SJB pickups.

The tight, focused bass and punchy, clear treble are purely and powerfully output by the Duncan pickups.

The sound is an open and clear tone, truly capturing the essence of a 70s Jazz Bass.

The powerful, punchy attack when fingerpicking, as well as the crisp, responsive feel when slapping, are major attractions.

It also boasts excellent rigidity and playability thanks to reliable Japanese woodworking techniques, allowing you to directly experience the rich, full tone inherent in passive basses.

Don't miss this opportunity!
